# Sightline

> A dependency-free TypeScript charting library that renders 100k+ data points at 60fps on a
> `<canvas>` while staying fully accessible — keyboard-navigable, screen-reader-announced,
> find-in-page-able — through a real-DOM overlay. Because the data lives in the DOM (not just
> canvas pixels), the same layer makes charts **machine-readable to AI agents**. Web platform
> only (browsers); ~12KB gzip ESM, zero runtime dependencies.

This file is everything an agent needs to build on Sightline. Source of truth: the public
API below. Chart family today: time-series **line + area**, multiple series.

## Install

```sh
npm install sightline
```
```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/sightline/dist/sightline.umd.cjs"></script>
<script>const { Sightline } = window.Sightline;</script>
```

## Minimal usage

```ts
import { Sightline } from 'sightline';

// The container must have a non-zero size (e.g. width:100%; height:420px). Styles auto-inject.
const chart = new Sightline(document.getElementById('chart'), {
  series: [
    { name: 'Price', color: '#16a34a', type: 'area' },
    { name: 'VWAP', color: '#d97706' },
  ],
  options: { ariaLabel: 'Sensor telemetry', xLabel: 'time', yLabel: 'value' },
});

// x is shared and non-decreasing; one y per series, each the same length as x.
const N = 100_000;
const x = Float64Array.from({ length: N }, (_, i) => i);
chart.setData({ x, y: [seriesA, seriesB] }); // seriesA/B: Float64Array | number[] of length N
```

## Public API

```ts
new Sightline(el: HTMLElement, config: SightlineConfig)

interface SightlineConfig {
  series: SeriesConfig[];
  data?: SightlineData;          // optional; or call setData later
  options?: SightlineOptions;
}

interface SeriesConfig {
  name: string;                  // announced to SR, shown in legend/tooltip
  color: string;                 // CSS color
  type?: 'line' | 'area';        // default 'line'
  visible?: boolean;             // default true
  width?: number;                // stroke px, default 1.25
  fillAlpha?: number;            // area fill 0..1, default 0.15
}

interface SightlineData {
  x: Float64Array | readonly number[];        // shared, NON-DECREASING, finite
  y: (Float64Array | readonly number[])[];    // one per series, each length === x.length, finite
}

interface SightlineOptions {
  ariaLabel?: string;            // accessible chart name (also the summary label)
  xLabel?: string; yLabel?: string;
  legend?: boolean;              // default true
  maxDpr?: number;               // device-pixel-ratio cap, default 2
  yPadding?: number;             // y-extent padding fraction, default 0.06
  xInteger?: boolean;            // integer-stepped x ticks, default false
  xTickCount?: number;           // default 8
  yTickCount?: number;           // default 6
  formatX?: (v: number) => string;  // tick + readout x formatter
  formatY?: (v: number) => string;  // tick + readout y formatter
  reducedMotion?: boolean;       // else auto from prefers-reduced-motion
  highContrast?: boolean;        // else auto from prefers-contrast
}

// Methods (setData/update/renderSync return `this` for chaining)
chart.setData(data: SightlineData): Sightline      // replace data, reset view to full x-domain
chart.update(patch: Partial<SightlineConfig>): Sightline  // patch series/options/data in place
chart.renderSync(domain?: readonly [number, number]): Sightline  // synchronous render / programmatic zoom
chart.summary(): ChartSummary                      // structured machine-readable summary (see below)
chart.destroy(): void                              // remove DOM, listeners, observers, timers
chart.renderPath: 'dom-overlay' | 'html-in-canvas' // getter; DOM-overlay is the default, no-flags path
chart.htmlInCanvas: { supported: boolean; via: string | null } // getter

interface ChartSummary {
  label: string; points: number; xStart: number; xEnd: number;
  series: SeriesSummary[];
}
interface SeriesSummary {
  name: string; visible: boolean;
  min: number; max: number; first: number; last: number; mean: number;
  changeAbs: number;   // last - first
  changePct: number;   // percent change vs first (range-relative when first is 0)
  trend: 'up' | 'down' | 'flat';  // derived from changePct; <1% is flat
}
```

## Agent-readable: how an AI agent reads the data

A canvas chart is opaque pixels. Sightline puts the data in the DOM, so an agent can read it
three ways (all inside the chart container):

1. **JS, if you hold the instance:** `chart.summary()` → `ChartSummary`.
2. **Embedded JSON, from the DOM:** `JSON.parse(container.querySelector('script[data-sightline]').textContent)` → the same `ChartSummary`.
3. **Accessibility tree:** the focusable `[role="application"]` surface has `aria-describedby`
   pointing to a one-line natural-language summary (values + trend), `aria-details` pointing to
   a hidden `<table class="sl-table-alt">` of downsampled rows, and an `[aria-live="polite"]`
   region that announces the focused sample. Axis ticks are real `.sl-tick` text.

To navigate values programmatically: focus the surface, dispatch ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down/Home/End,
and read the `[aria-live]` element's textContent after each step.

## Interaction model

- Wheel = zoom x around the pointer · drag = pan.
- Focus the `[role="application"]` surface, then: `ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight` move between samples,
  `ArrowUp`/`ArrowDown` switch series, `Home`/`End` jump to ends, hold `Shift` for fine (1-sample) steps.
- Legend buttons (`aria-pressed`) toggle series visibility.

## Theming

CSS custom properties on the container or `:root` (a few examples; all have sensible defaults):
`--sl-ink` (DOM text), `--sl-tick-color`, `--sl-grid`, `--sl-axis`, `--sl-cursor`, `--sl-ring`
(cursor-dot outline), `--sl-focus` (focus ring), `--sl-readout-bg/-ink/-border` (tooltip).

## Data contract & gotchas

- `x` must be non-decreasing; `x` and every `y` must be the same length; values should be finite
  (non-finite y is skipped from stats and renders as a gap).
- One shared `x` across all series (columnar). For irregular/clustered x the renderer still draws
  a gap-free envelope.
- The container needs an explicit size. Styles are injected once per document — no CSS import.
- HTML-in-Canvas is feature-detected; the DOM-overlay path is the default and works in stock
  Chrome/Firefox/Safari with no flags.

## Performance

- Render cost is O(viewport width), independent of point count: a precomputed min/max pyramid is
  picked per frame so per-frame work tracks pixels, not N. Frame cost is ~flat from 10k to 250k
  points (measured ratio ~1.2x; <16ms budget with large headroom).
- `setData` is O(N) once (builds the pyramid + stats); rendering never iterates raw N.

## Scope (today) and what's NOT included

- IN: time-series line + area, multiple series; wheel-zoom/drag-pan; keyboard cursor; legend;
  the accessibility + agent-readable layer.
- NOT YET: candlestick/OHLC/bars/heatmap, real-time streaming appends, framework adapters
  (React/Vue/Svelte), SSR. The renderer-agnostic `core/` (scales, ticks, pyramid, scheduler)
  is platform-agnostic, so a WebGL backend or a React Native (Skia) renderer can slot in later,
  but they are not built. This is a web library — it does not run natively in React Native as-is.

## Links

- Quickstart + theming: README.md
- Live demo + benchmark vs uPlot/SVG: bench/ (run `pnpm bench`), landing/ (run `pnpm landing`)
- Honest validation results: FINDINGS.md
